sources.role: deprecations + Vertex batch tiers + policy flags

money: integer micro-USD per 1M tokens (USD/Mtok * 1e6); null = not offered / priced-per-host
note: Direct first-party authoritative layer. Complements config/model-registry.json (OpenRouter-derived). On conflict, this layer's official-page citations win. Cross-check `flags` rows before treating any price as billing-grade. See docs/registry/RECONCILIATION.md for cross-source agreement + discrepancies.

routing_policy_flags.allow_batch: Vertex Flex/Batch (e.g. gemini-2.5-pro 0.625/5.00 vs 1.25/10.00 standard) — batch semantics only
routing_policy_flags.allow_promo: time-boxed promo pricing (e.g. deepseek-v4-pro until 2026-05-31 15:59 UTC) — expires
routing_policy_flags.allow_aggregator: third-party hosts of open weights (Together/Fireworks/Novita/...) vs first-party
routing_policy_flags.allow_free: free tiers / free routes (Groq, Cerebras, OpenRouter :free, Z.ai Flash) — non-SLA only

flags: agent read 2.00/6.00; Oracle x2 + Bedrock parity = 0.50/1.50

spread.note: Tight band; Groq adds cache-hit discount + speed.
~9x input spread for identical weights — host choice dominates.
Direct API ~4-5x cheaper than aggregator markup (promo).
Direct and Novita tie on headline; DeepSeek has 10x cheaper cache.
Z.ai direct ties with Bedrock on headline token price.
Direct Mistral ties with Bedrock; both 0.50/1.50 (Oracle x2).
Direct cheaper on input; aggregators add convenience.
Aggregator can undercut the first party.
